payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.description: Claim pocket money task completion

payload.justification: Signal SG906 describes the strategic process of making earnings and achievements visible to children.

payload.justification: Signal SG907 identifies the strategic process of delivering weekly transparency for financial awareness.

payload.justification: Signal SG908 describes the strategic validation process parents perform as part of oversight accountability.

payload.justification: Signal SG908 identifies disbursement as a strategic process parents perform following validation.

payload.justification: Signal SG909 describes the strategic process of maintaining historical records for accountability and trend analysis.

payload.description: Parents must have access to child-earnings-tasks reports for payment fulfillment

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Household Participation Framework
payload.description: Enterprise concern defining who participates in household economic activities and their respective roles. Establishes the fundamental relationship between children as earning participants and parents as authority figures responsible for governance and oversight of the compensation framework.

payload.name: Compensation Stewardship
payload.description: Enterprise accountability for establishing, maintaining, and governing the compensation structure that determines economic value of contributions. Encompasses the authority to define what activities merit compensation, their relative worth, and the criteria for earning eligibility within the household economy.

payload.name: Opportunity Transparency
payload.description: Enterprise commitment to ensure participants have complete visibility into available earning opportunities and their achievements. Addresses the obligation to make economic participation accessible and understandable to all household members through clear communication of possibilities and outcomes.

payload.name: Performance Recognition
payload.description: Enterprise responsibility for acknowledging and recording completion of activities that constitute claims against the household economy. Encompasses the process by which contributions are formally recognized, validated through oversight authority, and transformed into compensation obligations.

payload.name: Financial Obligation Discharge
payload.description: Enterprise accountability for fulfilling validated compensation commitments to participants. Represents the obligation to transfer economic value once performance claims have been substantiated, completing the cycle of contribution and reward within the household economy.

payload.name: Historical Accountability
payload.description: Enterprise commitment to preserve complete records of economic activity, enabling retrospective oversight, trend analysis, and verification of compensation practices. Establishes the foundation for demonstrating fair treatment and supporting long-term pattern assessment across the household economy.

payload.name: Task Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for defining, making available, and maintaining the persistent record of household tasks including their monetary values and completion states across lifecycle boundaries.

payload.name: Task Completion Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing and recognizing when tasks reach completed state, enabling children to claim accountability for work performed and triggering downstream entitlements.

payload.name: Earnings Entitlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing the relationship between completed tasks and monetary entitlement, aggregated across weekly business cycles, ensuring children can view their earned amounts and parents can fulfill payment obligations.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to participate in household economic activities through task-based compensation.
payload.rationale: Combines the enterprise goal of enabling earning opportunities with the recognition of children as participants and household activities as the scope of compensated work.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise children as participants entitled to claim completion of household tasks.
payload.rationale: Establishes the structural relationship of children as a recognised class of participant with a defined entitlement within the household framework.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish parental authority to define compensation terms and provide oversight.
payload.rationale: Asserts the governance constraint that parents hold definitional and supervisory authority within the framework.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a task-based compensation structure.
payload.rationale: The enterprise is accountable for creating the foundational structure that governs how tasks are defined and valued for compensation purposes.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain completion-based earning opportunities.
payload.rationale: The enterprise commits to providing ongoing earning opportunities tied to task completion within the household economy.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall govern the determination of task value within the compensation structure.
payload.rationale: Parental oversight and accountability require governance authority over how economic value is assigned to contributions.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall ensure parental accountability for compensation decisions.
payload.rationale: Enterprise-level governance obligation that parental authority remains accountable for all compensation-related determinations.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide visibility into available earning opportunities to all participants.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to ensure earning opportunities are visible to household members, particularly children, enabling informed economic participation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide visibility into participant achievements.
payload.rationale: Enterprise obligation to make participant accomplishments and outcomes transparent, complementing opportunity visibility.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide earning transparency on a weekly basis.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to regular, predictable communication of earnings aligned with the weekly task completion and earning cycle.
payload.fit_criteria: Earning information is communicated at least once per week.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable child financial awareness through visibility of earning opportunities.
payload.rationale: Enterprise policy commitment to support child development objectives by ensuring earning opportunities serve an educational purpose in building financial literacy.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall support participant motivation through visibility of earning opportunities.
payload.rationale: Enterprise policy to ensure transparency mechanisms serve motivational goals, reinforcing engagement through clear communication of possibilities.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a process for recognising the completion of activities that constitute claims against the household economy.
payload.rationale: The enterprise must formally recognise task completion events as the foundation for compensation claims. Process-column CCI combined with completion event triggers functional responsibility.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall validate contributions that have been recognised as completed before establishing compensation obligations.
payload.rationale: Validation by oversight authority is a distinct functional step between recognition and compensation obligation. Validation event distinguishes this from initial recognition.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain records of completed achievements that establish the evidentiary basis for compensation claims.
payload.rationale: Entity-column content describing completed achievement record represents a structural commitment to maintain this information entity at enterprise scope. Object-recursion from recognition process surfaces this structural requirement.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall disburse earned compensation to participants.
payload.rationale: Combines the process obligation to disburse (How-006) with the object of earned compensation (What-005), forming a single atomic enterprise-level commitment to complete the compensation cycle once validation has occurred.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain longitudinal records of economic activity to enable retrospective oversight and verification of compensation practices.
payload.rationale: Consolidates the enterprise commitment to preserve historical records (How), the existence of longitudinal records supporting accountability (What), and the motivating purpose of enabling accountability and trend analysis (Why) into a single normative obligation at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: Parents shall define household tasks.
payload.rationale: Business capability for the parent role to create and specify tasks as business entities; derived from the task entity definition, the task definition function, and the parent actor responsibility.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of the monetary value associated with each task.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to record and maintain the monetary value attribute as part of the task entity; derived from the What-column attribute CCI.

payload.statement: The business shall make available to children a view of available tasks.
payload.rationale: Business capability to provide visibility of available tasks to child participants; derived from the task visibility function and the constraint ensuring children can view tasks.

payload.statement: The business shall make available to children a view of completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Business capability to provide visibility of completed tasks to child participants; split from available-task viewing as they represent distinct visibility obligations.

payload.statement: The business shall reset task availability on weekly cycle boundaries.
payload.rationale: Business capability to govern the temporal lifecycle of task availability based on weekly cycles; derived from How-column business rule governing temporal behaviour.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of historical task data.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to maintain persistent historical task records; derived from the historical task entity and the retention rule, reframed as business stewardship obligation rather than technical storage.

payload.statement: The business shall enable children to claim accountability for tasks they have completed.
payload.rationale: HOW-column process capability combined with WHO-column actor identification establishes the business responsibility to provide a claiming mechanism for completed tasks.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise task completion as the trigger enabling child claiming capability.
payload.rationale: WHEN-column event defines the business responsibility to establish the temporal relationship between task completion state and downstream claiming capability.

payload.statement: The business shall account for monetary rewards due to children for completed tasks.
payload.rationale: WHY-column goal articulates the business obligation to maintain accountability for compensation arising from completed work; 'account for' captures the business-level stewardship responsibility without dropping to technical vocabulary.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of the relationship between each child actor, their completed tasks, and the resulting earnings.
payload.rationale: WHAT-column relationship entity and WHO-column child actor together establish the business responsibility to maintain the foundational earnings entitlement record linking children to their work and compensation.

payload.statement: The business shall aggregate earnings on a weekly basis.
payload.rationale: WHAT-column weekly earnings entity combined with WHEN-column weekly business cycle establishes the temporal aggregation capability the business must provide.

payload.statement: Weekly earnings shall be structured as an aggregated business entity.
payload.rationale: WHAT-column entity expressing the structural nature of weekly earnings as an aggregated construct at the business level.

payload.statement: The business shall make available earnings reports showing the relationships between children, tasks, and earnings to support payment fulfillment.
payload.rationale: HOW-column earnings reporting function combined with WHO-column parent actor establishes the business capability to provide reporting for payment obligations.

payload.statement: The business shall make weekly earnings visible to the child actor who earned them.
payload.rationale: WHY-column visibility constraint combined with WHO-column child actor establishes a governance rule requiring earnings transparency to children.

payload.statement: The business shall make child-earnings-tasks reports accessible to parent actors for payment fulfillment purposes.
payload.rationale: WHY-column access constraint combined with WHO-column parent actor establishes a governance rule requiring report availability to enable parents to meet payment obligations.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Requirement elements present in the ledger.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the core purpose of enabling children to claim completion-based pocket money tasks throughout a weekly cycle.
Articulates the parental motivation to define task-based compensation structures for household activities.
Reflects the motivational goal of providing children visibility into earning opportunities and completed achievements.
Defines the purpose of providing weekly earning transparency to support child financial awareness.
Establishes parental oversight and accountability objectives for validating and disbursing earned compensation.
Indicates strategic intent to maintain longitudinal records supporting accountability and trend analysis.
Section header indicating the beginning of a conceptual system overview.
Defines the core business purpose as enabling children to claim completed tasks for monetary reward.
Establishes that parents define task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Specifies the conceptual relationship between completed tasks and child actors.
Defines visibility requirements for children to view available and completed task entities.
Establishes the business concept of weekly earnings aggregation visible to child actors.
Defines parental reporting capability showing child-earnings-tasks relationships for payment fulfillment.
Establishes the business rule that task availability resets on a weekly cycle boundary.
Requires retention of historical task data as a persistent business entity concept.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Signal elements present in the ledger.
This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Stakeholder elements present in the ledger.
